reasoning: Based on Wikipedia data as of 2025-10-26, Lilly King holds the women's 100m breaststroke world record at 1:04.13 (set in July 2017). Recent competition results show the current generation of top swimmers is significantly off this pace - the 2025 World Championships winner (Anna Elendt) recorded 1:05.19, over 1 second slower than King's record. With no major international long course competitions scheduled before the resolution dates and current performance gaps remaining substantial, the probability of the record standing is high for all dates. However, uncertainty increases with time horizon as domestic meets or breakthrough performances could occur.
